other_observers: Nick Komar, Andrew Spencer, Gary Lefko, Eric Defonso; Nick Komar originally found the bird earlier in the day.
specific_location: On a small private lake below, east of, one of the dams of Horsetooth Reservoir on the west side of Fort Collins. The lake is called College Lake.
description: It was a small goose. It had an all black head, and neck down to the breast. Also a small black bill. It also had black on the belly and on the edge of the tail. The back also has black on it, but it is not as dark of black as the rest of the bird. The rest of the tail, and upper and undertail coverts are white. It had a small line of white on the side of the bird and it has a small line of white on its neck. The reason I don't believe this is a full adult is becasue there should be more white on the neck and sides of the bird.Behaviors: The bird was hanging out with Canada and Cackling Geese. It was in the water the whole time swimming around the small lake.Call: Didn't hear it.Plumage: Black close to adult
photographer: Photographer: Cole Wild, Cole Wild, Cole Wild
